commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of compatibility-sensitive upgrade/provider semantics, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff uses the existing trusted official plugin install path and adds no dependencies, workflows, permissions, or credential handling surfaces. (sha=8bb2c3d5169622f5723bf31f9e3fa633a432cbbe)
commands.review_summary:
The PR refreshes stale managed @openclaw/codex runtime installs during doctor repair, avoids repeated beta-channel refreshes, and maps bare codex/gpt-* status routes to Codex OAuth usage.

PR surface: Source +104, Tests +377. Total +481 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/proof level: current main lacks the stale version-bound Codex package check and only maps harness=codex plus provider=openai for status usage; no live current-main repro was run because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of compatibility-sensitive upgrade/provider semantics, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(codex): refresh stale managed runtime plugin
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the Codex stale-plugin convergence path behind https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650, including the duplicate evidence from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120.

What changes:

- Refreshes stale version-bound managed `@openclaw/codex` runtime packages through the trusted configured-plugin repair path when the installed package version is older than the running OpenClaw version.
- Leaves newer managed Codex packages alone so doctor does not downgrade a local/future install.
- Treats same-release beta-channel `@openclaw/codex@beta` companion packages as converged so repeated `doctor --fix` runs do not reinstall them.
- Restores `/status` usage lookup for bare `codex/gpt-*` routes by mapping Codex harness status auth through `openai-codex`.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120. ClawSweeper closed that issue as a duplicate and asked that its stale-plugin-version, providerIds, config-backup, screenshot, and `codex/gpt-*` status evidence be folded into #87650.

Related but separate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87851 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87436. That PR handles logical OpenAI session-route persistence after Codex-backed runs. This PR does not touch that persistence surface and is not intended as the repair for issue #87436. The changes are complementary: #87851 keeps durable session route state canonical, while this PR repairs stale managed Codex runtime package installs and the `codex/gpt-*` status/auth display path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale managed `@openclaw/codex` packages could survive an OpenClaw update and leave the Codex runtime package behind the main app version, matching the reported `provider is not one of codex` failure evidence. A bare `codex/gpt-*` workaround route could also lose the Codex usage/auth status line because status looked up `codex` instead of the `openai-codex` auth provider.
- Real environment tested: Mantis native Telegram Desktop proof against this PR's `/status` path, plus local Windows source worktree proof on current PR head `99405a3b7c0d834ec0c0845b56caa5eab94d2dda` rebased onto current `main` through https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f6e1bc393b39c637e4b116b0d7b702676de5c869, including https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88137 and later main-CI fixes. No live VPS/user install was changed for this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Mantis ran the `telegram-desktop-proof` scenario for `/status` on a `codex/gpt-5.5` route; local verification ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`, `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`, changed-file `oxlint`, changed-file `oxfmt --check`, and `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix: Mantis proof comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88135#issuecomment-4580013044 and artifact https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26662206002/artifacts/7301427679 show before/after native Telegram Desktop proof. Candidate proof shows the Codex OAuth status line restored for `/status` on `codex/gpt-5.5`; main proof omits that line. Motion clips are linked from the Mantis comment.
- Observed result after fix: native Telegram Desktop proof shows the candidate status card includes the Codex OAuth status line for the affected route. Focused doctor/status regression tests passed, the beta-channel second doctor pass now converges without reinstalling `@openclaw/codex@beta`, adjacent doctor Codex route warning tests passed, current-main import-cycle check passed, changed-file lint passed, formatting check passed, and whitespace diff check passed.
- Installed/package doctor proof after fix: captured on 2026-05-29 from a disposable local npm install of this PR package, without touching my live VPS/user install. I packed PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0`, installed the tarball into an isolated npm prefix, configured an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, set `update.channel=beta` and `agents.defaults.model=openai/gpt-5.5`, seeded a real stale managed `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` npm payload plus install record, then ran `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive`.
- Evidence from that installed/package proof: `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (d082b78)`. Before doctor, the `codex` install record and installed `package.json` were both `2026.5.6`. Doctor reported `Installed missing configured plugin "codex" from @openclaw/codex@beta`. After doctor, the `codex` install record had `source=npm`, `spec=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedName=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedVersion=2026.5.28-beta.4`, `resolvedSpec=@openclaw/codex@2026.5.28-beta.4`, and the installed package payload was `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.28-beta.4`.
- Follow-up convergence proof after ClawSweeper review: on source head `99405a3b7c0d834ec0c0845b56caa5eab94d2dda`, added regression coverage where the first beta-channel doctor pass repairs stale `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` to `2026.5.28-beta.4`, then a second doctor pass makes no install call and returns no changes.
- Note on package channel: at capture time npm reported `@openclaw/codex` dist-tags `latest=2026.5.27` and `beta=2026.5.28-beta.4`; beta channel was used so the managed Codex runtime package refreshed onto the current beta companion line for the PR package.
- What was not tested: no live VPS/user install was changed for this installed-package proof; the disposable temp state, npm prefix, and npm cache were deleted after capture.

## Verification

Passed locally after the latest follow-up commit:

- `src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`: 63 tests passed, including beta-channel second-pass convergence.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`: 23 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`: 17 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`: 104 tests passed.
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`: 0 cycles.
- `pnpm exec oxlint ...changed files...`: passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...changed files...`: passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`: passed.

Known local proof gap:

- `src/plugins/update.test.ts` has one unrelated local Windows failure in `reuses the recorded managed extensions root when updating external plugins`; no files in that test surface are changed by this PR.
target.author: brokemac79
